name: Soapwort Root
description: The richest known natural source of saponins, acting as amphiphilic surfactants that gently cleanse hair and skin at the scalp's natural pH (~5).
chemicalName: Triterpenoid saponins (saponarioside A–D, quillaic acid glycosides)
appearance: Dried root: pale tan to reddish-brown, fibrous; Decoction: slightly foamy amber liquid
solubility: Saponins: soluble in water and aqueous ethanol; poorly soluble in non-polar solvents
chemistry: Richest natural saponin source (224–694 mg/g). Amphiphilic surfactant at pH ~5 (matches scalp). Cleans without stripping lipid barrier. Antibacterial.
warnings: Toxic if ingested. Lethal to fish. Irritates eyes. Spoils 7–10 days. Use distilled water.
